review_notes: The functions provided are applicable to Kubernetes and align well with the CCCS CM-7 control focused on least functionality. The naming conventions are mostly followed correctly, and the functions map accurately to Kubernetes security controls. However, a minor improvement could be made for clarity in the 'k8s_pod_security_standard_restricted' function to specify the standard being enforced.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_pod_security_standard_restricted' to 'k8s_pod_security_standard_restricted_policy' for clarity on the specific policy being enforced.
title: CCCS CM-7: Least Functionality
step1_reasoning: The control CCCS CM-7 focuses on ensuring least functionality, which in a Kubernetes context translates to restricting network access, limiting permissions, and enforcing strict security standards. NetworkPolicies can be used to ensure that ingress and egress traffic is denied by default, aligning with the principle of least functionality. RBAC can enforce least privilege by ensuring users and service accounts have only the permissions they need. Pod security standards can restrict pods from having excessive capabilities or running in privileged modes.
